articles.title: 'Just being cute, funny': Donald Trump clarifies 'I'm the boss' remark at G7 summit
articles.description: US President Donald Trump has clarified his "I'm the boss" remark at the G7 summit, stating it was a lighthearted joke intended to be funny, not an assertion of authority.  He explained the comment arose from the unusual seating arrangement at the summit.  Trump's clarification comes amid significant diplomatic developments, including a US-Iran agreement.

articles.title: 'Major win for India': Vivek Aggarwal elected as FATF vice president
articles.description: India has achieved a significant leadership role in the Financial Action Task Force (FATF) with Vivek Aggarwal elected as its Vice President. This appointment underscores India's commitment to combating money laundering and terrorist financing globally. Aggarwal's extensive experience, including leading India's FATF delegation and heading the Financial Intelligence Unit, will bolster the organization's efforts to secure financial integrity and dismantle illicit financial systems.

articles.title: 'Thought it was terrorist explosion': London train crash passengers 'had broken legs, could not speak'; driver dead
articles.description: A tragic train collision near Bedford claimed the life of a driver and left over 80 injured, with survivors recounting scenes of panic and devastation. Passengers described being violently thrown, witnessing smoke, and hearing screams as emergency services rushed to the site. The incident involved two East Midlands Railway services heading towards London St Pancras, leading to a complete suspension of services.

articles.title: Accenture outlook cut sparks fresh fears over IT recovery
articles.description: Global tech spending concerns have intensified as Accenture lowered its FY26 revenue growth forecast to 3-4%. This outlook, despite a resilient third quarter, signals potential prolonged weak demand for Indian IT services firms, possibly extending into FY27. Analysts suggest this weakness at a leading IT player negatively impacts demand prospects for India's top IT companies, hinting at a slower start to the next fiscal year.

articles.title: Rupee ends higher for second day, settles at 94.33 as trade deal hopes offset oil concerns
articles.description: The Indian rupee strengthened for a second day, closing at 94.33 against the US dollar, buoyed by positive India-US trade talks. Hopes for an interim trade agreement between the two nations are rising, with negotiators urged to expedite efforts. However, global uncertainties and a stronger dollar limited the rupee's gains. Analysts anticipate continued inflows supporting the local currency.

articles.title: ODI tickets worth Rs 52.36 lakh went unsold: Khanna
articles.description: "Tickets worth Rupees 52,36,000 were unsold. Why all the tickets were not sold-out? DDCA started online ticket sale quite late that too without proper advertisement," Khanna claimed in a media release.

articles.title: JAC to close JTET 2026 registrations today for Primary and Upper Primary aspirants; check direct link here
articles.description: Jharkhand TET 2026: The Jharkhand Academic Council (JAC) will close the online application process for JTET 2026 today, June 20, 2026, following an extension of the registration deadline. The state-level eligibility examination is conducted for candidates seeking teaching eligibility for Primary and Upper Primary schools in Jharkhand. The notification was released on March 28, 2026, and applications began on April 21. The examination will be held in offline mode, with the date to be announced later.
articles.full_text: Jharkhand TET 2026: The Jharkhand Academic Council (JAC) will close the online application process for JTET 2026 today, June 20, 2026, following an extension of the registration deadline. The state-level eligibility examination is conducted for candidates seeking teaching eligibility for Primary and Upper Primary schools in Jharkhand. The notification was released on March 28, 2026, and applications began on April 21. The examination will be held in offline mode, with the date to be announced later.

articles.title: BITSAT 2026 seat allotment result out today, counselling process moves to next stage
articles.description: The BITSAT 2026 Iteration 1 seat allotment result has been released, marking the next stage of the admission process for candidates seeking admission to BITS Pilani, Goa and Hyderabad campuses. Applicants can check their allotment status through the official admission portal using their login credentials. Candidates offered seats must complete fee payment by June 26, 2026. Those not allotted seats in the first round will remain eligible for consideration in subsequent counselling iterations.

articles.title: MET 2026 Round 2 allotment result today; candidates must confirm admission by June 25
articles.description: MAHE will release the MET 2026 Round 2 seat allotment result on June 20 after 8 PM through its official counselling portal. Candidates can check their allotment status by logging in with their application number and password. The Round 2 cutoff will be published alongside the allotment list. Candidates allotted seats must complete the first instalment fee payment and confirm admission by June 25. Fresh registrations remain available for each counselling round.

articles.title: No IAS, no UPSC: How America's bureaucracy works without a permanent elite cadre
articles.description: India's IAS-led bureaucracy is often compared with the United States, where no UPSC-style examination or IAS equivalent exists. Instead, a new US President can appoint roughly 4,000 political officials, while leadership positions listed in the government's Plum Book number around 8,000–9,000. However, administrative continuity is maintained by a permanent federal workforce of more than 20 lakh civilian employees. The article explains how America's two-layered system balances political change with bureaucratic stability.

The article explains how America's two-layered system balances political change with bureaucratic stability.

articles.title: Aamir-Gauri to have intimate registered marriage
articles.description: Bollywood star Aamir Khan is set to tie the knot for the third time on July 5th with Gauri Spratt. The actor confirmed that the wedding will be an intimate registered ceremony at home, attended only by close family and friends. Following the nuptials, Khan is expected to return to filming for Rajkumar Hirani's '3 Idiots 2', a sequel to the beloved 2009 hit.

articles.title: X review for ‘Voicemails for Isabelle’
articles.description: A new romantic comedy, 'Voicemails for Isabelle,' is captivating audiences on Netflix, quickly topping viewership charts. The film, starring Zoey Deutch and Nick Robinson, follows a woman coping with grief by leaving voicemails for her deceased sister, unknowingly sending them to a Texas real estate agent. Viewers are praising the lead actors' chemistry and the movie's blend of humor and emotion, reminiscent of classic 2000s rom-coms.

articles.title: Diljit honoured with 'Diljit Dosanjh Day' by LA city council
articles.description: Los Angeles has officially declared January 6, 2027, as 'Diljit Dosanjh Day' in a special civic honour. The Los Angeles City Council recognized the Punjabi singer and actor for his significant role in globalizing Punjabi music and championing South Asian culture in mainstream American entertainment. Councilwoman Monica Rodriguez introduced the resolution, marking a major milestone in Dosanjh's international career. Fans celebrated the news online, praising the artist's achievements.

articles.title: Pax spotted at Brad Pitt's family event, despite strained relationship
articles.description: Despite most of Angelina Jolie's children distancing themselves from Brad Pitt, Pax Jolie-Pitt is reportedly keeping connections with his paternal family. He was recently seen at a family engagement party for cousin Sydney Pitt. This development highlights ongoing tensions within the family, especially as reports indicate other siblings are opting to drop 'Pitt' from their names following the divorce.
